name: Adaptability
effect: Increases the Same Type Attack Bonus from *1.5 to *2.

name: Aerilate
effect: Normal-type moves become Flying-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

name: Aftermath
effect: Damages the attacker if it knocks out the Pokémon with a move that makes direct contact.

name: Air Lock
effect: Eliminates the effects of weather.

name: Analytic
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's move if it is the last to act that turn.

name: Anger Point
effect: The Pokémon is angered when it takes a critical hit, and that maxes its Attack stat.

name: Anger Shell
effect: When an attack causes its HP to drop to half or less, the Pokémon gets angry. This lowers its Defense and Sp. Def stats but boosts its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats.

name: Anticipation
effect: The Pokémon can sense an opposing Pokémon's dangerous moves.

name: Arena Trap
effect: Prevents opposing Pokémon from fleeing from battle.

name: Armor Tail
effect: The mysterious tail covering the Pokémon's head makes opponents unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

name: Aroma Veil
effect: Protects the Pokémon and its allies from effects that prevent the use of moves.

name: As One
effect: This Ability combines the effects of both Calyrex's Unnerve Ability and Glastrier's Chilling Neigh Ability.

name: As One
effect: This Ability combines the effects of both Calyrex's Unnerve Ability and Spectrier's Grim Neigh Ability.

name: Aura Break
effect: The effects of “Aura” Abilities are reversed to lower the power of affected moves.

name: Bad Dreams
effect: Damages opposing Pokémon that are asleep.

name: Ball Fetch
effect: If the Pokémon is not holding an item, it will fetch the Poké Ball from the first failed throw of the battle.

name: Battery
effect: Powers up ally Pokémon's special moves.

name: Battle Armor
effect: Hard armor protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

name: Battle Bond
effect: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, its bond with its Trainer is strengthened, and its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats are boosted.

name: Beads of Ruin
effect: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous beads lowers the Sp. Def stats of all Pokémon except itself.

name: Beast Boost
effect: The Pokémon boosts its most proficient stat each time it knocks out a Pokémon.

name: Berserk
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat when it takes a  hit that causes its HP to become half or less.

name: Big Pecks
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from having its Defense stat lowered.

name: Blaze
effect: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

name: Bulletproof
effect: Protects the Pokémon from some ball and bomb moves.

name: Cheek Pouch
effect: The Pokémon's HP is restored when it eats any Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.

name: Chilling Neigh
effect: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a chilling neigh, which boosts its Attack stat.

name: Chlorophyll
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in harsh sunlight.

name: Clear Body
effect: Prevents other Pokémon's moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon's stats.

name: Cloud Nine
effect: Eliminates the effects of weather.

name: Color Change
effect: The Pokémon’s type becomes the type of the move used on it.

name: Comatose
effect: The Pokémon is always drowsing and will never wake up. It can attack while in its sleeping state.

name: Commander
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it goes inside the mouth of an ally Dondozo if one is on the field. The Pokémon then issues commands from there.

name: Competitive
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat sharply when its stats are lowered by an opposing Pokémon.

name: Compound Eyes
effect: The Pokémon's compound eyes boost its accuracy.

name: Contrary
effect: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them—and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

name: Corrosion
effect: The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.

name: Costar
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it copies an ally's stat changes.

name: Cotton Down
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around and lowers the Speed stat of all Pokémon except itself.

name: Cud Chew
effect: When the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will regurgitate that Berry at the end of the next turn and eat it one more time.

name: Curious Medicine
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it scatters medicine from its shell, which removes all stat changes from allies.

name: Cursed Body
effect: May disable a move that has dealt damage to the Pokémon.

name: Cute Charm
effect: The Pokémon may infatuate attackers that make direct contact with it.

name: Damp
effect: The Pokémon dampens its surroundings, preventing all Pokémon from using explosive moves such as Self-Destruct.

name: Dancer
effect: Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.

name: Dark Aura
effect: Powers up each Pokémon’s Dark-type moves.

name: Dauntless Shield
effect: Boosts the Pokémon’s Defense stat the first time the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Dazzling
effect: The Pokémon dazzles its opponents, making them unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

name: Defeatist
effect: Halves the Pokémon’s Attack and Sp. Atk stats when its HP becomes half or less.

name: Defiant
effect: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

name: Delta Stream
effect: The Pokémon changes the weather to eliminate all of the Flying type’s weaknesses.

name: Desolate Land
effect: The Pokémon changes the weather to nullify Water-type attacks.

name: Disguise
effect: Once per battle, the shroud that covers the Pokémon can protect it from an attack.

name: Download
effect: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.

name: Dragonize
effect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.

name: Dragon’s Maw
effect: Powers up Dragon-type moves.

name: Drizzle
effect: The Pokémon makes it rain when it enters a battle.

name: Drought
effect: Turns the sunlight harsh when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Dry Skin
effect: Restores the Pokémon's HP in rain or when it is hit by Water-type moves. Reduces HP in harsh sunlight, and increases the damage received from Fire-type moves.

name: Early Bird
effect: The Pokémon awakens from sleep twice as fast as other Pokémon.

name: Earth Eater
effect: If hit by a Ground-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

name: Eelevate
effect: The Pokémon floats off the ground, making it immune to Ground-type moves, as well as the Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Sticky Web statuses. When the Pokémon knocks out a target with an attack, its highest stat is boosted by 1 stage.

name: Effect Spore
effect: Contact with the Pokémon may inflict poison, sleep, or paralysis on the attacker.

name: Electric Surge
effect: Turns the ground into Electric Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Electromorphosis
effect: The Pokémon becomes charged when it takes damage, boosting the power of the next Electric-type move the Pokémon uses.

name: Embody Aspect
effect: The Pokémon's heart fills with memories, causing the Mask to shine and one of the Pokémon's stats to be boosted.

name: Emergency Exit
effect: The Pokémon, sensing danger, switches out when its HP becomes half or less.

name: Fairy Aura
effect: Powers up every Pokémon in play's Fairy-type moves by 33%

name: Filter
effect: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

name: Fire Mane
effect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Fire-type moves by 50%.

name: Flame Body
effect: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

name: Flare Boost
effect: Powers up special moves when the Pokémon is burned.

name: Flash Fire
effect: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

name: Flower Gift
effect: Boosts the Attack and Sp. Def stats of itself and allies in harsh sunlight.

name: Flower Veil
effect: Ally Grass-type Pokémon are protected from status conditions and the lowering of their stats.

name: Fluffy
effect: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

name: Forecast
effect: The Pokémon transforms with the weather to change its type to Water, Fire, or Ice.

name: Forewarn
effect: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can tell one of the moves an opposing Pokémon has.

name: Friend Guard
effect: Reduces damage dealt to allies.

name: Frisk
effect: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

name: Full Metal Body
effect: Prevents other Pokémon’s moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon’s stats.

name: Fur Coat
effect: Halves the damage from physical moves.

name: Gale Wings
effect: Gives priority to the Pokémon's Flying-type moves while its HP is full.

name: Galvanize
effect: Normal-type moves become Electric-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

name: Gluttony
effect: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

name: Good as Gold
effect: A body of pure, solid gold gives the Pokémon full immunity to other Pokémon's status moves.

name: Gooey
effect: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

name: Gorilla Tactics
effect: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat but only allows the use of the first selected move.

name: Grass Pelt
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Defense stat on Grassy Terrain.

name: Grassy Surge
effect: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Grim Neigh
effect: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a terrifying neigh, which boosts its Sp. Atk stat.

name: Guard Dog
effect: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat if intimidated. Moves and items that would force the Pokémon to switch out also fail to work.

name: Gulp Missile
effect: When the Pokémon uses Surf or Dive, it will come back with prey. When it takes damage, it will spit out the prey to attack.

name: Guts
effect: It's so gutsy that having a status condition boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat.

name: Hadron Engine
effect: Turns the ground into Electric Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle. The futuristic engine within the Pokémon also boosts its Sp. Atk stat on Electric Terrain.

name: Harvest
effect: May create another Berry after one is used.

name: Healer
effect: Sometimes cures the status conditions of the Pokémon's allies.

name: Heatproof
effect: The Pokémon's heatproof body halves the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

name: Heavy Metal
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's weight.

name: Honey Gather
effect: The Pokémon may gather Honey after a battle.

name: Hospitality
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring a small amount of the ally's HP

name: Huge Power
effect: The Pokémon's Attack stat is doubled while it has this ability.

name: Hunger Switch
effect: The Pokémon changes its form, alternating between its Full Belly Mode and Hangry Mode after the end of every turn.

name: Hustle
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat but lowers its accuracy.

name: Hydration
effect: Cures the Pokémon's status conditions in rain.

name: Hyper Cutter
effect: The Pokémon's prized, mighty pincers prevent other Pokémon from lowering its Attack stat.

name: Ice Body
effect: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in snow.

name: Ice Face
effect: The Pokémon's ice head can take a physical attack as a substitute, but the attack also changes the Pokémon's appearance. The ice will be restored when it snows.

name: Ice Scales
effect: The Pokémon is protected by ice scales, which halve the damage taken from special moves.

name: Illuminate
effect: By illuminating its surroundings, the Pokémon prevents its accuracy from being lowered.

name: Illusion
effect: The Pokémon fools opponents by entering battle disguised as the last Pokémon in its Trainer's party.

name: Immunity
effect: The Pokémon's immune system prevents it from being poisoned.

name: Imposter
effect: The Pokémon transforms itself into the Pokémon it's facing.

name: Infiltrator
effect: The protections and stat boosts caused by the moves Substitute, Reflect, Light Screen and Safeguard by the opponent are ignored.

name: Innards Out
effect: Damages the attacker landing the finishing hit by the amount equal to its last HP.

name: Inner Focus
effect: The Pokémon's intense focus prevents it from flinching or being affected by Intimidate.

name: Insomnia
effect: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

name: Intimidate
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it intimidates opposing Pokémon and makes them cower, lowering their Attack stats.

name: Intrepid Sword
effect: Boosts the Pokémon’s Attack stat the first time the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Iron Barbs
effect: Inflicts damage on the attacker upon contact with iron barbs.

name: Iron Fist
effect: The power of punching moves is increased by 20%. This stacks with

name: Justified
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a Dark-type attack, its Attack stat is boosted by its sense of justice.

name: Keen Eye
effect: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

name: Klutz
effect: The Pokémon can't use any held items.

name: Leaf Guard
effect: Prevents status conditions in harsh sunlight.

name: Levitate
effect: Damage dealing Ground-type moves have no effect on this Pokémon. Cannot be trapped by Arena Trap ability. Takes no damage from Spikes.

name: Libero
effect: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

name: Light Metal
effect: Halves the Pokémon's weight.

name: Lightning Rod
effect: The Pokémon draws in all Electric-type moves. Instead of taking damage from them, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted.

name: Limber
effect: The Pokémon's limber body prevents it from being paralyzed.

name: Lingering Aroma
effect: Contact with the Pokémon changes the attacker's Ability to Lingering Aroma.

name: Liquid Ooze
effect: The strong stench of the Pokémon's oozed liquid damages attackers that use HP-draining moves.

name: Liquid Voice
effect: Sound-based moves become Water-type moves.

name: Long Reach
effect: The Pokémon uses its moves without making contact with the target.

name: Magic Bounce
effect: Reflects non-attacking moves used on the Pokémon back to the attacking Pokémon

name: Magic Guard
effect: The Pokémon only takes damage from attacks.

name: Magician
effect: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

name: Magma Armor
effect: The Pokémon’s hot magma coating prevents it from being frozen.

name: Magnet Pull
effect: Prevents Steel-type Pokémon from fleeing by pulling them in with magnetism.

name: Marvel Scale
effect: The Pokémon's marvelous scales boost its Defense stat if it has a status condition.

name: Mega Launcher
effect: Powers up pulse moves.

name: Mega Sol
effect: Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.

name: Merciless
effect: The Pokémon's attacks become critical hits if the target is poisoned.

name: Mimicry
effect: Changes the Pokémon’s type depending on the terrain.

name: Mind’s Eye
effect: The Pokémon ignores changes to opponents' evasiveness, its accuracy can't be lowered, and it can hit Ghost types with Normal-type and Fighting-type moves

name: Minus
effect: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

name: Mirror Armor
effect: Bounces back only the stat-lowering effects that the Pokémon receives.

name: Misty Surge
effect: Turns the ground into Misty Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Mold Breaker
effect: The Pokémon's moves are not affected by foe’s abilities during battle

name: Moody
effect: Every turn, one of the Pokémon's stats will be boosted sharply but another stat will be lowered.

name: Motor Drive
effect: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Electric-type moves. Instead, its Speed stat is boosted.

name: Moxie
effect: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it shows moxie, which boosts its Attack stat.

name: Multiscale
effect: Reduces damage afflicted to the Pokémon by 50% if at maximum HP

name: Multitype
effect: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the plate it holds.

name: Mummy
effect: Contact with the Pokémon changes the attacker’s Ability to Mummy.

name: Mycelium Might
effect: The Pokémon will always act more slowly when using status moves, but these moves will be unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

name: Natural Cure
effect: The Pokémon's status conditions are cured when it switches out.

name: Neuroforce
effect: Powers up moves that are super effective.

name: Neutralizing Gas
effect: While the Pokémon is in the battle, the effects of all other Pokémon's Abilities will be nullified or will not be triggered.

name: No Guard
effect: The accuracy of all moves known by this Pokémon and all Pokémon targeting this Pokémon raises to 100%

name: Normalize
effect: All the Pokémon’s moves become Normal type. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

name: Oblivious
effect: The Pokémon is oblivious, keeping it from being infatuated, falling for taunts, or being affected by Intimidate.

name: Opportunist
effect: If an opponent's stat is boosted, the Pokémon seizes the opportunity to boost the same stat for itself.

name: Orichalcum Pulse
effect: Turns the sunlight harsh when the Pokémon enters a battle. The ancient pulse thrumming through the Pokémon also boosts its Attack stat in harsh sunlight.

name: Overcoat
effect: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms. It is also protected from the effects of powders and spores.

name: Overgrow
effect: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

name: Own Tempo
effect: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

name: Parental Bond
effect: Parent and child each attacks.

name: Pastel Veil
effect: Protects the Pokémon and its ally Pokémon from being poisoned.

name: Perish Body
effect: When hit by a move that makes direct contact, the Pokémon and the attacker will faint after three turns unless they switch out of battle.

name: Pickpocket
effect: The Pokémon steals the held item from attackers that made direct contact with it.

name: Pickup
effect: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

name: Piercing Drill
effect: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.

name: Pixilate
effect: Normal-type moves become Fairy-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

name: Plus
effect: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

name: Poison Heal
effect: If poisoned, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

name: Poison Point
effect: Contact with the Pokémon may poison the attacker.

name: Poison Puppeteer
effect: Pokémon poisoned by Pecharunt's moves will also become confused.

name: Poison Touch
effect: May poison a target when the Pokémon makes contact.

name: Power Construct
effect: Other Cells gather to aid when its HP becomes half or less. Then the Pokémon changes its form to Complete Forme.

name: Power Spot
effect: Just being next to the Pokémon powers up moves.

name: Power of Alchemy
effect: The Pokémon copies the Ability of a defeated ally.

name: Prankster
effect: Gives priority to the Pokémon's status moves.

name: Pressure
effect: Puts other Pokémon under pressure, causing them to expend more PP to use their moves.

name: Primordial Sea
effect: The Pokémon changes the weather to nullify Fire-type attacks.

name: Prism Armor
effect: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks taken.

name: Propeller Tail
effect: Ignores the effects of opposing Pokémon's Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

name: Protean
effect: Before the Pokémon uses a move, it becomes a pure Pokémon of that type. This only works once per switch in.

name: Protosynthesis
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

name: Psychic Surge
effect: Turns the ground into Psychic Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

name: Punk Rock
effect: Boosts the power of sound-based moves. The Pokémon also takes half the damage from these kinds of moves.

name: Pure Power
effect: Using its pure power, the Pokémon doubles its Attack stat.

name: Purifying Salt
effect: The Pokémon's pure salt protects it from status conditions and halves the damage taken from Ghost-type moves.

name: Quark Drive
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

name: Queenly Majesty
effect: Opponents are unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

name: Quick Draw
effect: Enables the Pokémon to move first occasionally.

name: Quick Feet
effect: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon has a status condition.

name: RKS System
effect: Changes the Pokémon’s type to match the memory disc it holds.

name: Rain Dish
effect: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in rain.

name: Rattled
effect: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

name: Receiver
effect: The Pokémon copies the Ability of a defeated ally.

name: Reckless
effect: Powers up moves that have recoil damage.

name: Refrigerate
effect: Normal-type moves become Ice-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

name: Regenerator
effect: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

name: Ripen
effect: Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.

name: Rivalry
effect: The Pokémon's competitive spirit makes it deal more damage to Pokémon of the same gender, but less damage to Pokémon of the opposite gender.

name: Rock Head
effect: Protects the Pokémon from recoil damage.

name: Rocky Payload
effect: Powers up Rock-type moves.

name: Rough Skin
effect: The Pokémon's rough skin damages attackers that make direct contact with it.

name: Run Away
effect: Enables a sure getaway from wild Pokémon.

name: Sand Force
effect: Boosts the power of Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves in a sandstorm.

name: Sand Rush
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in a sandstorm.

name: Sand Spit
effect: The Pokémon creates a sandstorm when it's hit by an attack.

name: Sand Stream
effect: The Pokémon summons a sandstorm when it enters a battle.

name: Sand Veil
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

name: Sap Sipper
effect: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

name: Schooling
effect: When it has a lot of HP, the Pokémon forms a powerful school. It stops schooling when its HP is low.

name: Scrappy
effect: The Pokémon can hit Ghost-type Pokémon with Normal- and Fighting-type moves. It is also unaffected by Intimidate.

name: Screen Cleaner
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, the effects of Light Screen, Reflect, and Aurora Veil are nullified for both opposing and ally Pokémon.

name: Seed Sower
effect: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.

name: Serene Grace
effect: Raises the likelihood of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses its moves.

name: Shadow Shield
effect: Reduces the amount of damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full.

name: Shadow Tag
effect: The Pokémon steps on the opposing Pokémon's shadows to prevent them from fleeing or switching out.

name: Sharpness
effect: Powers up slicing moves.

name: Shed Skin
effect: The Pokémon may cure its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

name: Sheer Force
effect: Removes any additional effects from the Pokémon's moves, but increases the moves' power.

name: Shell Armor
effect: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

name: Shield Dust
effect: Protective dust shields the Pokémon from the additional effects of moves.

name: Shields Down
effect: When its HP drops to half or less, the Pokémon's shell breaks and it becomes aggressive.

name: Simple
effect: Doubles the effects of the Pokémon's stat changes.

name: Skill Link
effect: Maximizes the number of times multistrike moves hit.

name: Slow Start
effect: For five turns, the Pokémon's Attack and Speed stats are halved.

name: Slush Rush
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in snow.

name: Sniper
effect: If the Pokémon's attack lands a critical hit, the attack is powered up even further.

name: Snow Cloak
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in snow.

name: Snow Warning
effect: Snowstorm blows when the Pokémon enters battle. As of X & Y, this lasts 5 turns.

name: Solar Power
effect: In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat is boosted, but its HP decreases every turn.

name: Solid Rock
effect: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

name: Soul-Heart
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat every time another Pokémon faints.

name: Soundproof
effect: Soundproofing gives the Pokémon full immunity to all sound-based moves.

name: Speed Boost
effect: The Pokémon's Speed stat is boosted every turn.

name: Spicy Spray
effect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, it burns the attacker.

name: Stakeout
effect: Doubles the damage dealt to a target that has just switched into battle.

name: Stall
effect: The Pokémon is always the last to use its moves.

name: Stalwart
effect: Ignores the effects of opposing Pokémon's Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

name: Stamina
effect: Boosts the Defense stat when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.

name: Stance Change
effect: The Pokémon changes its form to Blade Forme when it uses an attack move and changes to Shield Forme when it uses King’s Shield.

name: Static
effect: The Pokémon is charged with static electricity and may paralyze attackers that make direct contact with it.

name: Steadfast
effect: The Pokémon's determination boosts its Speed stat every time it flinches.

name: Steam Engine
effect: Boosts the Speed stat drastically when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire- or Water-type move.

name: Steelworker
effect: Powers up Steel-type moves.

name: Steely Spirit
effect: Powers up the Steel-type moves of the Pokémon and its allies.

name: Stench
effect: By releasing a stench when attacking, the Pokémon may cause the target to flinch.

name: Sticky Hold
effect: The Pokémon's held items cling to its sticky body and cannot be removed by other Pokémon.

name: Storm Drain
effect: The Pokémon draws in all Water-type moves. Instead of taking damage from them, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted.

name: Strong Jaw
effect: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

name: Sturdy
effect: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

name: Suction Cups
effect: The Pokémon uses suction cups to stay in one spot. This protects it from moves and items that would force it to switch out.

name: Super Luck
effect: The Pokémon is so lucky that the critical-hit ratios of its moves are boosted.

name: Supersweet Syrup
effect: Lowers the evasion of opposing Pokémon by 1 stage when first sent into battle

name: Supreme Overlord
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, its Attack and Sp. Atk stats are slightly boosted for each of the allies in its party that have already been defeated.

name: Surge Surfer
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat on Electric Terrain.

name: Swarm
effect: Powers up Bug-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

name: Sweet Veil
effect: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

name: Swift Swim
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

name: Sword of Ruin
effect: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous sword lowers the Defense stats of all Pokémon except itself.

name: Symbiosis
effect: The Pokémon passes its held item to an ally that has used up an item.

name: Synchronize
effect: If the Pokémon is burned, paralyzed, or poisoned by another Pokémon, that Pokémon will be inflicted with the same status condition.

name: Tablets of Ruin
effect: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous wooden tablets lowers the Attack stats of all Pokémon except itself.

name: Tangled Feet
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness if it is confused.

name: Tangling Hair
effect: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

name: Technician
effect: Powers up weak moves so the Pokémon can deal more damage with them.

name: Telepathy
effect: The Pokémon anticipates and dodges the attacks of its allies.

name: Tera Shell
effect: The Pokémon's shell contains the powers of each type. All damage-dealing moves that hit the Pokémon when its HP is full will not be very effective.

name: Tera Shift
effect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it absorbs the energy around itself and transforms into its Terastal Form.

name: Teraform Zero
effect: When Terapagos changes into its Stellar Form, it uses its hidden powers to eliminate all effects of weather and terrain, reducing them to zero.

name: Teravolt
effect: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

name: Thermal Exchange
effect: Boosts the Attack stat when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire-type move. The Pokémon also cannot be burned.

name: Thick Fat
effect: The Pokémon is protected by a layer of thick fat, which halves the damage taken from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

name: Tinted Lens
effect: The Pokémon can use “not very effective” moves to deal regular damage.

name: Torrent
effect: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

name: Tough Claws
effect: Powers up moves that make direct contact.

name: Toxic Boost
effect: Powers up physical moves when the Pokémon is poisoned.

name: Toxic Chain
effect: The power of the Pokémon's toxic chain may badly poison any target the Pokémon hits with a move

name: Toxic Debris
effect: Scatters poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team when the Pokémon takes damage from physical moves.

name: Trace
effect: Ability becomes the same as that of the opponent. Switching this Pokémon out of battle restores its original ability. In a Double Battle, a random opponent’s ability will be copied.

name: Transistor
effect: Powers up Electric-type moves.

name: Triage
effect: Gives priority to the Pokémon's healing moves.

name: Truant
effect: Each time the Pokémon uses a move, it spends the next turn loafing around.

name: Turboblaze
effect: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

name: Unaware
effect: When attacking, the Pokémon ignores the target's stat changes.

name: Unburden
effect: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon's held item is used or lost.

name: Unnerve
effect: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

name: Unseen Fist
effect: The Pokémon can deal damage with moves that make physical contact, even if the target is protected.

name: Vessel of Ruin
effect: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous vessel lowers the Sp. Atk stats of all Pokémon except itself.

name: Victory Star
effect: Boosts the accuracy of its allies and itself.

name: Vital Spirit
effect: The Pokémon is full of vitality, and that prevents it from falling asleep.

name: Volt Absorb
effect: If hit by an Electric-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

name: Wandering Spirit
effect: The Pokémon exchanges Abilities with a Pokémon that hits it with a move that makes direct contact.

name: Water Absorb
effect: If hit by a Water-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

name: Water Bubble
effect: Lowers the power of Fire-type moves that hit the Pokémon and prevents it from being burned.

name: Water Compaction
effect: Boosts the Defense stat sharply when the Pokémon is hit by a Water-type move.

name: Water Veil
effect: The Pokémon's water veil prevents it from being burned.

name: Weak Armor
effect: The Pokémon's Defense stat is lowered when it takes damage from physical moves, but its Speed stat is sharply boosted.

name: Well-Baked Body
effect: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Fire-type moves. Instead, its Defense stat is sharply boosted.

name: White Smoke
effect: The Pokémon is protected by its white smoke, which prevents other Pokémon from lowering its stats.

name: Wimp Out
effect: The Pokémon cowardly switches out when its HP becomes half or less.

name: Wind Power
effect: The Pokémon becomes charged when it is hit by a wind move, boosting the power of the next Electric-type move the Pokémon uses.

name: Wind Rider
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat if Tailwind takes effect or if the Pokémon is hit by a wind move. The Pokémon also takes no damage from wind moves.

name: Wonder Guard
effect: Its mysterious power only lets supereffective moves hit the Pokémon.

name: Wonder Skin
effect: Makes status moves more likely to miss the Pokémon.

name: Zen Mode
effect: Changes the Pokémon’s shape when HP is half or less.

name: Zero to Hero
effect: The Pokémon transforms into its Hero Form when it switches out.

name: Madrugar
effect: El Pokémon se despierta del sueño el doble de rápido que otros Pokémon.

name: Geofagia
effect: Si recibe un movimiento de tipo Tierra, el Pokémon recupera PS en lugar de recibir daño.

name: Electrolevitación
effect: El Pokémon flota sobre el suelo, volviéndose inmune a los movimientos de tipo Tierra, así como a los efectos de Púas, Púas Tóxicas y Red Viscosa. Cuando debilita a un objetivo con un ataque, su estadística más alta aumenta un nivel.

name: Efecto Espora
effect: El contacto con el Pokémon puede causar envenenamiento, sueño o parálisis al atacante.

name: Electrogénesis
effect: Convierte el terreno en Campo Eléctrico cuando el Pokémon entra en combate.

name: Electromorfosis
effect: El Pokémon se carga de electricidad al recibir daño, aumentando la potencia del siguiente movimiento de tipo Eléctrico que utilice.

name: Aspecto Encarnado
effect: El corazón del Pokémon se llena de recuerdos, haciendo que su máscara brille y aumentando una de sus estadísticas.

name: Retirada
effect: Cuando sus PS se reducen a la mitad o menos, el Pokémon percibe el peligro y se retira del combate.

name: Aura Feérica
effect: Aumenta en un 33% la potencia de los movimientos de tipo Hada de todos los Pokémon en combate.

name: Filtro
effect: Reduce el daño recibido de los ataques supereficaces.

name: Melena Ígnea
effect: Aumenta en un 50% la potencia de los movimientos de tipo Fuego del Pokémon.

name: Cuerpo Llama
effect: El contacto con el Pokémon puede causar quemaduras al atacante.

name: Ímpetu Ardiente
effect: Potencia los movimientos especiales cuando el Pokémon está quemado.

name: Absorbe Fuego
effect: Si recibe un movimiento de tipo Fuego, el Pokémon absorbe las llamas y aumenta la potencia de sus propios movimientos de tipo Fuego.

name: Don Floral
effect: Aumenta el Ataque y la Defensa Especial del Pokémon y sus aliados bajo luz solar intensa.

name: Velo Flor
effect: Protege a los Pokémon de tipo Planta aliados frente a problemas de estado y reducciones de estadísticas.

name: Peluche
effect: Reduce a la mitad el daño recibido por movimientos de contacto, pero duplica el daño recibido de movimientos de tipo Fuego.

name: Predicción
effect: El Pokémon cambia de forma con el clima, alterando su tipo a Agua, Fuego o Hielo.

name: Alerta
effect: Cuando entra en combate, el Pokémon puede identificar uno de los movimientos del rival.

name: Compiescolta
effect: Reduce el daño recibido por los aliados.

name: Cacheo
effect: Cuando entra en combate, el Pokémon puede comprobar el objeto equipado del rival.

name: Armadura Metálica
effect: Evita que los movimientos o habilidades de otros Pokémon reduzcan las estadísticas del Pokémon.

name: Pelaje Recio
effect: Reduce a la mitad el daño recibido de movimientos físicos.

name: Alas Vendaval
effect: Otorga prioridad a los movimientos de tipo Volador mientras el Pokémon tenga todos sus PS.

name: Piel Eléctrica
effect: Los movimientos de tipo Normal se convierten en movimientos de tipo Eléctrico. La potencia de esos movimientos aumenta ligeramente.

name: Gula
effect: Si el Pokémon lleva una Baya que se activa con pocos PS, la consumirá cuando sus PS bajen a la mitad o menos.

name: Cuerpo Áureo
effect: El cuerpo de oro puro del Pokémon le otorga inmunidad total a los movimientos de estado de otros Pokémon.

name: Baba
effect: El contacto con el Pokémon reduce la Velocidad del atacante.

name: Monotema
effect: Aumenta el Ataque del Pokémon, pero solo le permite usar el primer movimiento seleccionado.

name: Manto Herboso
effect: Aumenta la Defensa del Pokémon cuando el Campo de Hierba está activo.

name: Herbogénesis
effect: Convierte el terreno en Campo de Hierba cuando el Pokémon entra en combate.

name: Relincho Negro
effect: Cuando el Pokémon debilita a un objetivo, emite un relincho aterrador que aumenta su Ataque Especial.

name: Perro Guardián
effect: Aumenta el Ataque del Pokémon si es intimidado. Además, los movimientos y objetos que obligarían al Pokémon a retirarse fallan.

name: Tragamisil
effect: Cuando el Pokémon usa Surf o Buceo, regresa con una presa. Si recibe daño, escupirá la presa para atacar.

name: Agallas
effect: El coraje del Pokémon aumenta su Ataque cuando sufre un problema de estado.

name: Motor Hadrónico
effect: Convierte el terreno en Campo Eléctrico cuando el Pokémon entra en combate. El motor futurista del Pokémon también aumenta su Ataque Especial mientras el Campo Eléctrico esté activo.

name: Cosecha
effect: Puede generar otra Baya después de haber consumido una.

name: Alma Cura
effect: A veces cura los problemas de estado de los Pokémon aliados.

name: Ignífugo
effect: El cuerpo resistente al calor del Pokémon reduce a la mitad el daño recibido de movimientos de tipo Fuego.
